Title: Yunara
Age: Current1561 – 1566 years old Timeline Born570 BN – 565 BN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
As a child, Yunara's parents entrusted her to the Kinkou Order to be raised under their tutelage. She trained under Master Bhinan, the Eye of Twilight, and Master Lao, the Fist of Shadow, who wielded a sacred relic known as the Aion Er'na. By the time she matured into an acolyte, a scourge had begun to plague the land, and the Kinkou prepared for war. Corrupted god-warriors from Shurima, known as the Darkin, had invaded the First Lands, masquerading as benevolent Kanmei.

The Kinkou fought back against the invader gods and their armies but suffered heavy losses. One evening, Yunara saved Master Lao from one of the fallen one's followers, before he realized the true nature of their foe. He smeared the blood from her hands onto his robes and told her to run, before facing Xolaani and perishing. Yunara took up his duty as the Fist of Shadow and inherited the Aion Er'na.

The Kinkou were eventually pushed back until only their stronghold in Koeshin remained. As they struggled to defend it, they were saved by another god-warrior, one who was uncorrupted. He met with the Kinkou's leaders and introduced himself as Zaahen, explaining that he had come to Ionia to end the bloodshed caused by his siblings. He offered his aid, and though the Kinkou were wary of accepting it, they had no alternative and agreed to an alliance after Bhinan judged his intentions.

Zaahen shared arcane teachings from Shurima, revealing how to defeat and seal the Darkin, and for nearly three years he fought alongside the Kinkou against the invader gods. During this time, Yunara was assigned to monitor Zaahen and, if necessary, eliminate him should he betray them. Together, they ultimately defeated and sealed Varus, Rhaast, Xolaani, and 9 Praa.

After the last Darkin had been defeated, Bhinan judged Zaahen once more and deemed him safe to depart in peace. However, Zaahen felt that he too was succumbing to the darkness and feared what he might become. He voluntarily sealed himself within his glaive, which was then kept within the Temple of Twilight. Yunara swore to be his guardian and was cast into the Spirit Realm as well, relinquishing her role as the Fist of Shadow.

To help their nation heal, the Kinkou spun Yunara and Zaahen's tale into a wholesome fable about how a shrine maiden and a mighty spirit fought back against malevolent pretenders in the War of the False Kanmei.

During their confinement within the Spirit Realm, Yunara often visited the Temple of Twilight, though she never crossed its threshold. Zaahen believed she wished to free him, but they both knew she could not. His presence often attracted azakana to the temple, but Yunara would repel them. Being in the Spirit Realm reminded her of the departed—Master Lao often coming to mind, along with the lessons he had taught her. With endless time to train, Yunara experimented with non-combat applications for the Aion Er'na, learning to dance with them and using them as stepping stones between her chambers and the temple.

One day, while meditating, Yunara experienced a vision of a stranger dressed in foreign robes. Surprised, Yunara pondered its meaning, as the temple was warded against all intrusions, even dreams.

children.title: Return to the Material Realm
children.textContent:
Centuries later, a disturbance in the Spirit Realm sent Yunara and the Temple of Twilight back into the material realm. The temple's sudden return sent the local demonkin into a frenzy, causing them to attack the nearby town of Koeshin. Yunara left her post to repel the demonic assault and encountered Xin Zhao, who was also fighting the demons. Xin was surprised to meet another Kinkou, having believed that only Kennen was in the area. Yunara asked to speak with her old friend, but Xin informed her that Kennen was currently conferring with Master Shen, the current Eye of Twilight. Though wary of her, Xin agreed to fight beside Yunara after she assured him that her skill in battle would earn his trust.

Together, they made their way through the forest, dispatching demons along the path. They encountered a strange creature— Atakhan—lurking in the woods but chose to leave it be. They eventually defeated a demonic pretender who had taken on the visage of the Grovemother, though Xin Zhao's spear was destroyed in the conflict. With the demons defeated, they regrouped with Kennen, who was shocked to see Yunara returned and her post abandoned. Soon after, they were alerted that the Temple of Twilight’s defenses had been breached and hurried back toward it.

As they made their way to the temple, Xin Zhao was plagued by a vision of Demacia in ruins. Unable to go further, he demanded to know what they were facing. Kennen was hesitant to reveal the truth, but relented after Yunara vouched for him. They explained that Zaahen laid within the temple and recounted how he had helped the Kinkou repel the Darkin invaders centuries ago. Kennen and Yunara urged Xin to return to his ship and warn Demacia that the Darkin have returned, but he refused to abandon them.

By the time they reached the temple, it had already retreated back into the Spirit Realm, with Atakhan inside. With the Spirit Realm being the Eye of Twilight's domain and Master Shen still far away, Xin Zhao and Yunara resolved to undergo the Trials of Twilight to reach the temple. As the Kinkou armorers outfitted Xin Zhao, Yunara explained that the trials were a series of tests created by the temple to judge the honor and strength of those who entered. Once they were prepared, Kennen opened a portal to the Spirit Realm.

The two were separated and forced to complete their own trials. When they finally reunited, Xin Zhao revealed that Zaahen had warned him not to take up a Darkin weapon, allowing him to pass his trial. Surprised that Zaahen had intervened, Yunara believed there was still good left in her old friend, and the two rushed onward to save him.

When they finally reached Zaahen's glaive, it was too late, the weapon had already been claimed by LeBlanc and embedded into Atakhan's shadowy flesh. The two charged the demon in an effort to free Zaahen, but Atakhan's power was overwhelming. Yunara used the Aion Er'na to pummel the creature while Xin Zhao shielded her from LeBlanc's chains. One chain managed to strike Yunara, causing her to falter and allowing the demon to bear down on them. As Atakhan assaulted them, Xin reached for the glaive to commune with the spirit trapped within. Inside Zaahen's mindscape, Xin pleaded for aid, but Zaahen refused, insisting that mortals should not meddle with the power of gods. Xin insisted they had no other choice and reminded Zaahen that he had once sacrificed himself to save their world, begging him to let him do the same. As Xin's strength waned, Zaahen finally relented and took him as his host. Returned to the world once more, Zaahen destroyed Atakhan with a single strike of his glaive, forcing LeBlanc to flee, while Yunara looked on.

children.title: Honoring Xin Zhao
children.textContent:
When Yunara returned to the material world, she decided to honor Xin Zhao's sacrifice by returning his spear to Demacia. As she made her way through the forests of Demacia, she was caught in a stampede. The beasts knocked her off her horse and Yunara was forced to run towards the Great City of Demacia. Upon reaching the Great City's borders, Prince Jarvan IV and a platoon of Silverwing riders dove forward and defeated the creatures. When the prince landed, Yunara presented him his uncle's spear.

Jarvan IV offered Yunara a ride on his Silverwing and flew her to the royal palace. He presented her to his father, King Jarvan III, and informed him of Xin Zhao's passing. Though Yunara had practiced what she would say, upon meeting the king she understood why Xin Zhao had dedicated his life to him. Jarvan III asked how Xin had fallen, and Yunara revealed that he had died fighting a demon. The king then brought her to Xin's quarters, explaining that he had chosen a room near the gardens because it reminded him of his homeland. He asked where Xin Zhao's body lay, but Yunara was unable to tell him that he had fallen to a Darkin. Seeing that she was mourning his loss as well, Jarvan told her that the burden was not hers alone and urged her to stay and see the nation that Xin Zhao had loved. As Yunara gazed upon Xin Zhao's armor, she prayed that Zaahen would prove worthy of his sacrifice.

Yunara remained in Demacia for several months, learning much about the kingdom, including its prejudice against magic. She attended Xin Zhao's funeral, where she witnessed Sword-Captain Tianna deliver a speech in his honor and unveil a statue of the fallen seneschal. After the funeral, Yunara decided to return to her homeland, but not before saying goodbye to King Jarvan and taking note of the apparent magical nature of Lux Crownguard and Lady Sona Buvelle.

Months later, Yunara began visiting the shrine the Kinkou Order had built in Xin Zhao's honor, where she would speak to her friend.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Yunara is a slender, fair-skinned woman with purple eyes and lavender hair. She wears elegant, traditional Ionian robes with a distinct palette of white, blue, and brown. Yunara wears ceremonial paint on her forehead.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
In ancient legend, Yunara was depicted as a kind and careful maiden who worried about her people and land, almost to the point of sacrificing herself for it.

Having been exiled to the Spirit Realm for more than 1000 years, Yunara has returned to an Ionia she does not recognize with modern customs she does not understand, and has also shown a lack of trust in the modern Kinkou Order's indecision and unwillingness to fight enemy invaders. As a fighter, her demeanor has become somewhat harsh towards both herself and others, but she remains faithful to her promises—the trait she became renowned for after the Darkin War. Yunara is also frequently hungry and believes a lack of food makes people "ornery".

children.title: Zaahen
children.textContent: Zaahen met Yunara when he came to help defend Ionia from the Darkin and their followers. Zaahen's power impressed the Kinkou, but also made them wary, so they assigned their Fist of Shadow Yunara to keep an eye on him and be ready to eliminate him if he betrayed them. In time, Yunara came to respect Zaahen as a trusted ally and hero of the First Lands, but he had grown to distrust himself and asked the Kinkou to seal him away in the Temple of Twilight, which was then hidden in the spirit realm. Yunara stood guard in the temple for over a thousand years as its protector to ensure Zaahen would not be freed by malevolent forces. When the temple was eventually breached by LeBlanc and Atakhan, Yunara and Xin Zhao went to go stop them, at first believing the Darkin had returned to free Zaahen. After her trial, in which Yunara heard Zaahen's voice, she decided there was still good in Zaahen and went to rescue him from the invaders.

children.title: Kennen
children.textContent: As they were both high-ranking members of the Kinkou Order, Yunara was old friends with Kennen and knew him before she went into her self-imposed exile. Yunara also promised she would stop Kennen if he ever lost his way.

children.title: Rhaast and  Varus
children.textContent: Rhaast and Varus were among the Darkin that Yunara fought and eventually defeated with the help of the Great Spirit.

children.title: Xin Zhao
children.textContent: Yunara met Xin Zhao during his pilgrimage to the Temple of Koeshin, where she and her shrine were unexpectedly returned to the mortal realm.

children.title: Syndra
children.textContent: Although she recognizes how dangerous she is, Yunara does not hate or fear Syndra; more than anything, she feels compassion for her after learning that it was the Ionians themselves who turned her into a wounded and explosive being.
